title: coccothrinax salvatoris subsp. loricata
intro: Coccothrinax salvatoris subsp. loricata, also known as the Cuban thatch palm, is a subspecies of palm tree native to Cuba. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The oil extracted from its seeds has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including oils and extracts, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions.
